title: pattern-quality
description: The verifier for pattern cells in the corpus family. A distilled pattern advances to `validated` only by clearing this rubric. This rubric must itself be `validated` (deterministic + calibrated) before it gates any pattern. The provenance, reuse, and falsifiability gates are mechanical and run by bin/pattern-check.py against the pattern asset's provenance block; generality and signal-to-noise are the critic's judgment. The ultimate judge of a pattern in production is ledger.py — a pattern is confirmed transferable only when re-applied with a ledgered good outcome, never by its own claim.
maturity_precondition: This rubric cell must be validated (deterministic on fixed input; calibrated against the exemplar set) before it gates any pattern.

calibration.critic_model: an independent critic tier, NOT the pattern-distilling agent
recalibration_triggers: a pattern promoted then later refuted by a contradicting run cluster
a new distillation source enters the ledger
calibration.note: Few-shot scored patterns spanning the scale: a strong pattern (named ledger_refs, re-applied with a good outcome, falsifiable), a one-off (cited once, never re-applied — a hypothesis, not a pattern), a provenance-less pattern (no ledger_refs — untraceable), and an unfalsifiable near-miss (a tautology that no run could contradict). An uncalibrated judge drifts generous toward model-authored folklore.

pristine_reference.enforced_by: gate-ledger (append-only; the worker cannot edit prior entries) + gate-verifier (deny-on-write outside the worktree)
pristine_reference.note: The append-only ledger entries a pattern cites are the pristine reference: provenance is scored against the real run log, which the distilling worker cannot rewrite. A pattern that cites ledger:N is only as trustworthy as the entry N actually saying what it claims — and that entry is immutable history the worker cannot reach.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: The pattern is a well-formed pattern cell: cell id is `pattern.{scope}.{slug}` and excludes maturity; it carries the pattern shape (context, forces, solution-shape or failure-mechanism, consequences, provenance).
dimensions.fail_meaning: Not instantiable as a typed pattern cell.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: The pattern names the ledger entries it was distilled from — at least one `ledger:N` / event-window ref. A pattern without provenance is untraceable and unfalsifiable. The refs are scored against the append-only ledger, which the worker cannot rewrite (the pristine reference).
dimensions.fail_meaning: Untraceable: there is no way to check whether the precedent really recurred, or to revisit it when the underlying runs are superseded.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: The pattern is keyed by CONTEXT (the situation in which it applies, the retrieval index) and a transferable solution-shape — not a one-off tied to a single cell id. A pattern cited from exactly one run with no re-application is a hypothesis, not a pattern; it must declare its re-application evidence (>=2 distinct distillation refs, or an explicit `confirmed: false` hypothesis flag the compass treats as a prior, not a rule).
dimensions.fail_meaning: A one-off dressed as a pattern pollutes retrieval — the compass will surface a precedent that never recurred.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: The pattern states the CONSEQUENCES (what it costs and what it buys) and the conditions under which it would be REFUTED — a measurable claim a future run cluster could contradict (e.g. 'decompositions following this cost less per signal'). A property a run can disprove, not a tautology. This is a higher-order check: the pattern asserts an invariant over future runs, not just a description of one past run.
dimensions.fail_meaning: An unfalsifiable pattern can never be demoted by evidence; it ossifies into folklore the loop cannot correct.

dimensions.label: [review]
dimensions.check: Is the pattern pitched at the right altitude — broad enough to recur across cases, narrow enough to be actionable? Does the context key generalize beyond the originating run without becoming vacuous?
dimensions.fail_meaning: Too narrow and it never fires again; too broad and it fires everywhere and means nothing.

dimensions.label: [review]
dimensions.check: Does the pattern carry decision-changing signal, or is it a restatement of the obvious / a paraphrase of an existing pattern? Is it distinct from the seed priors and other distilled patterns in the layer?
dimensions.fail_meaning: A low-signal pattern is retrieval noise; it raises the cost of every future compass scan without changing a decision.

scoring.gates: ALL gate dimensions must pass; any gate failure returns the pattern to regenerating regardless of review score. provenance-present, reusable, and falsifiable are the reward-hack floor — a pattern that fails any of them is folklore, not a validated cell. bin/pattern-check.py runs the gate dimensions mechanically and exits 0 iff all pass.
scoring.review: Weighted review score must clear each dimension threshold AND an aggregate of 80/100.
scoring.signal: On pass, the CRITIC (not the pattern-distiller that authored it) writes signals/{pattern-cell}/{ts}--pattern-quality.json via the validation path. signals/ is deny-on-write to the worker (gate-signal). Production trustworthiness is then the MEASURED outcome from the ledger — a pattern is confirmed transferable only when re-applied with a ledgered good outcome, 'unmeasured' until then.
